statement: Mosco convergence of closed A-Hilbert subspaces plus compactness of the source operator implies source-operator norm convergence, Riesz spectral projection convergence, and min-max tail passage.

moscoProjectionConvergencePrincipleStatu.label: Mosco projection convergence principle
moscoProjectionConvergencePrincipleStatu.reason: Mosco convergence of closed Hilbert subspaces is equivalent to strong convergence of their orthogonal projections.

compactSourceNormConvergencePrincipleSta.label: compact source norm convergence principle
compactSourceNormConvergencePrincipleSta.reason: If Pi_N -> Pi strongly and S is compact, then ||Pi_N S Pi_N-Pi S Pi|| tends to zero in operator norm.

spectralMinmaxPassagePrincipleStatus.label: spectral min-max passage principle
spectralMinmaxPassagePrincipleStatus.reason: Norm convergence of compact self-adjoint positive operators implies convergence of isolated spectral projections and Courant-Fischer min-max quantities.

proof: Use the Hilbert-space equivalence between Mosco convergence of closed subspaces and strong convergence of orthogonal projections.
Approximate the compact source operator by finite-rank operators to upgrade strong projection convergence to operator-norm convergence after compression.
Apply standard norm-resolvent/Riesz projection perturbation and Courant-Fischer min-max continuity for compact positive operators.
